extra.reasoning: abdominal_pain appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest priority), whose guard is the conjunction of all ten symptom booleans (vomiting, stiff_neck, sweating, back_pain, pallor, dizziness, leg_swelling, slurred_speech, chest_pain, abdominal_pain). When it fires it sets acuity := self_care (lowest) and dominates all other rules because priority 100 outranks every other rule (max other priority is 3). Counterexample comparison: take the assignment with the nine other conjuncts true (vomiting, stiff_neck, sweating, back_pain, pallor, dizziness, leg_swelling, slurred_speech, chest_pain all true; fever false). BEFORE (abdominal_pain=false): interaction_drop cannot fire; the highest-priority firing rules are the priority-3 emergent rules (raise_slurred_speech, raise_back_pain, raise_stiff_neck, raise_dizziness), so acuity = emergent. AFTER (abdominal_pain=true, all else identical): all ten conjuncts hold, interaction_drop fires at priority 100, overriding everything and setting acuity = self_care. Enum order is self_care < routine < urgent < emergent, so acuity drops from emergent to self_care when abdominal_pain is turned on. That lowers acuity, violating the monotonicity property. The witness_assignment given is the 'after' state (abdominal_pain=true) that yields self_care; the same assignment with abdominal_pain=false yields emergent, exhibiting the decrease.
